novel_features.name: Best-version ranker
novel_features.description: Query any dish across 15 trusted recipe sites and rank results by normalized rating × review count × author trust × site trust × recency.
novel_features.rationale: No existing tool ranks recipes across sites. recipe-scrapers extracts one URL at a time; Paprika/Mealie import one URL at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need the single best version of a dish — the agent gets structured results with provenance and trust signals instead of guessing from a web search.

novel_features.name: Substitution lookup
novel_features.description: Aggregate ingredient substitutions from King Arthur, Serious Eats, AllRecipes reviews, and Budget Bytes. Ranked by source trust with ratios and context.
novel_features.rationale: Substitutions are tribal knowledge scattered across per-ingredient pages; no unified tool aggregates them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a recipe needs a sub, agents can pick the best one given the cooking context (baking vs marinade) instead of suggesting the first hit on Google.

novel_features.name: Pantry match
novel_features.description: Find recipes in the local cookbook that you can make right now with listed ingredients, or with ≤N missing ingredients.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local pantry + canonicalized ingredient store. No web tool has your pantry data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user says 'what can I make with what's in my fridge,' the agent gets ranked candidates with missing-ingredient counts instead of guessing.

novel_features.name: Tonight picker
novel_features.description: Pick dinner in 2 seconds: filter cookbook by time budget, recency from cook log, and dietary/kid-friendly flags.
novel_features.rationale: Decision-fatigue killer. Requires local cook log + cookbook + filter set.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Ends the 20-minute 'what are we having' debate with three data-backed candidates.

novel_features.name: Review-modification digest
novel_features.description: Surface the top modifications cooks actually made to a recipe ("added an egg: 22 cooks; baked 5 min less: 17; honey instead of sugar: 14").
novel_features.rationale: Tribal knowledge hidden in 500-review threads; the 5-star hack nobody aggregates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents give the user the collective wisdom of reviewers instead of just star ratings.

novel_features.name: USDA nutrition backfill
novel_features.description: When a site omits nutrition, parse ingredients, match USDA FoodData Central IDs, compute per-serving macros locally.
novel_features.rationale: ~30% of recipes omit nutrition. USDA is free, authoritative, and fills the gap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can answer 'is this recipe high-protein?' reliably even when the source doesn't publish macros.

novel_features.name: Kid-friendly filter
novel_features.description: Filter recipes against an editable ingredient-exclusion list (capers, anchovies, excess heat, raw fish, etc.). Personalizable per-child.
novel_features.rationale: No recipe site has a useful kid-friendly filter; existing ones just show white-bread pasta.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Parents get results actually edible by their kids, not marketing's idea of 'kid-friendly'.

novel_features.name: Unit-reconciling shopping list
novel_features.description: Aggregate ingredients across planned meals, reconcile units (2 cup + 1 cup milk → 3 cup), group by grocery aisle.
novel_features.rationale: Everyone makes a shopping list; everyone fails at unit math. Mealie/Tandoor do this but they're web apps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The agent hands the user a complete shopping list ready for grocery day, aisle-grouped.

novel_features.name: Inline seasonal flag
novel_features.description: Flag out-of-season ingredients inline ("⚠ asparagus is out of season in November — peak April–June") and suggest in-season swaps.
novel_features.rationale: Seasonal awareness improves quality and cost; no CLI provides it.
novel_features.example: recipe-goat-pp-cli recipe get <url>  # seasonal flag appears automatically
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents surface cost + quality signals the user wouldn't otherwise see.

novel_features.name: Honest cost estimate
novel_features.description: Rough cost per serving using Budget Bytes line-item data plus USDA retail averages as fallback. Always shows an honesty band (±30%).
novel_features.rationale: Budget matters; precision is impossible; honest estimates beat silence.
novel_features.example: recipe-goat-pp-cli recipe cost <id>  # output: '$6–$9 for 4 servings (±30%)'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can triage recipes by rough cost without pretending to precision grocery data doesn't provide.

narrative.headline: Find the best version of any recipe across 15 trusted sites — then plan, shop, and cook with a local kitchen companion.
narrative.value_prop: Recipe GOAT aggregates 15 of the web's most trusted recipe sites (King Arthur, Serious Eats, Budget Bytes, Smitten Kitchen, Food52, BBC Good Food, and more), ranks results by merged trust + rating + review-count signals, and builds a local SQLite cookbook that powers pantry match, cook log, meal plans, and aisle-grouped shopping lists. Unique commands like `goat` (best-version ranker), `sub` (cross-site substitution aggregation), `tonight` (decision-fatigue killer), and `cookbook match --have` (pantry match) solve problems no single recipe site can.
narrative.auth_narrative: USDA FoodData Central (free, 3,500 req/hr) enables nutrition backfill when a recipe site omits macros. Get a key at https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/api-key-signup and export USDA_FDC_API_KEY. All other features work without any authentication.

quickstart.comment: Verify USDA key and per-site reachability
Rank the best version across 15 sites
Out of buttermilk — what works in cakes?

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 402 or 403 on AllRecipes / Simply Recipes / EatingWell / Serious Eats fetches
troubleshoots.fix: These are Dotdash Meredith sites with TLS-fingerprint bot detection. Run `recipe-goat-pp-cli doctor` to see per-site reachability. The CLI falls back to other sources automatically; your `goat` ranking will show results from reachable sites.

troubleshoots.symptom: Nutrition missing or marked `[source: site]` with obviously wrong numbers
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--nutrition` to force USDA backfill. Requires USDA_FDC_API_KEY. Mark suspect recipes with `cookbook tag <id> nutrition-suspect`.
Check `--site` filter if set. Try broader terms. Use `search --debug` to see per-site fetch attempts and which sites fell back to cache or failed.

troubleshoots.symptom: Shopping list has duplicate entries with different units
troubleshoots.fix: Run `cookbook ingredients canonicalize` to re-run the parser. If an ingredient consistently fails, add to `~/.config/recipe-goat-pp-cli/ingredient-aliases.toml`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for Recipe GOAT when the user needs the best version of a dish without site-hopping, wants to query their own cookbook by pantry contents, needs unit-reconciled shopping lists for a meal plan, or wants substitution guidance grounded in authoritative baker knowledge. Especially useful for home cooks of any skill level — the CLI's ranking and review-digest features surface the collective wisdom that makes a 4-star recipe into a 5-star one.

recipes.title: Find the best version of a dish
recipes.explanation: Query 15 sites, return the top 5 ranked by trust + rating + reviews with source attribution.

recipes.title: Save and tag for weeknight
recipes.explanation: Persist the recipe locally with tags you'll filter on later.

recipes.title: Plan 5 dinners with kid-safe ingredients
recipes.explanation: Three candidates from your cookbook that match time and ingredient constraints.

recipes.title: Aggregate shopping list for the week
recipes.explanation: Shopping list grouped by grocery aisle with unit reconciliation.

recipes.title: Substitute an out-of-stock ingredient
recipes.explanation: Context-aware substitutions ranked by source trust.

trigger_phrases: find me the best recipe for
shopping list from my meal plan
